title: Bridge Paper: Metacognition and mentalizing are associated with distinct neural representations of decision uncertainty.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

Paper: Metacognition and mentalizing are associated with distinct neural representations of decision uncertainty.
DOI: 10.1371/journal.pbio.3001301
Abstract: Metacognition and mentalizing are both associated with meta-level mental state representations. Conventionally, metacognition refers to monitoring one's own cognitive processes, while mentalizing refers to monitoring others' cognitive processes. However, this self-other dichotomy is insufficient to delineate the 2 high-level mental processes.
